reason: Response B is more vivid, better structured, and richer in concrete, knowledge-grounded sensory detail (the chlorophyll-withdrawal mechanism, atmospheric scattering explaining amber light, scent memory), building from color to light to texture/sound to a synthesized emotional conclusion. Response A is more abstract and noticeably repetitive, circling back to the "ending/beginning duality" and "transience" points multiple times with thinner sensory grounding, which reads as the smaller Haiku model. The greater control, layering, and specificity of B indicate Sonnet authorship.
